records.name: MEMPHIS, TN

console: MCI 416B 24-input — the same model used at Muscle Shoals Sound, Criteria (Miami), Willie Mitchell's Royal Studio, and Allen Toussaint's Sea-Saint Studio
MCI 536C mixing console (original; unchanged since Willie Mitchell era)

1969 Spectra Sonics (20×8, 3-band inductor EQ; 100% discrete; handcrafted by Welton Jetton — who also built consoles for Stax Records and Ardent Studios; one of the last Spectra Sonics/Auditronics builds; originally in Canada since 1969)
Sphere Eclipse A, 28-input, 16-bus (circa 1970s) — one of ~23–25 known to still exist; Class A transformer-balanced mic preamps; 910/920 graphic EQs; fully restored
tape: Studer A827 2" 24-track; MCI JH-110 ½" two-track
Scully 1" 8-track analog; Scully ¼" 2-track; Ampex 351 mono full-track (from estate of Les Paul); Pro Tools with Lynx Aurora converters
digital: Pro Tools HD 2; Pro Tools 192 I/O + 96 I/O; Sony PCM-2500 A/B DAT
Pro Tools 12; Lynx Aurora 16 + 8 (24 analog in/out @ 32-bit/192kHz)
Pro Tools; Logic Pro X; Ableton Live 10; UA Apollo 16 + 8
fields.iconic_outboard: 18× Universal Audio 101-D tube preamps (from the original Sam Phillips console); Gates SA39-B tube compressor; 2× Gates Sta-Level tube compressors; UA 176 + 2× UA 175b tube compressors; Teletronix LA-2A; UREI LA-3A; 2× DBX 160VU; 2× Pultec EQH-2; Hycor 4201 passive EQ; **EMT 140 stereo tube plate reverb**; UREI Cooper Time Cube delay
fields.echo_chambers: 3 live echo chambers **designed by Sam Phillips himself**
fields.microphones: 2× Neumann U48; Telefunken U47; Neumann FET 47; Neumann U67; 5× Neumann U87; RCA 77DX ribbon; Altec M11 "Coke Bottle" tube omni; AKG c451e pair; plus EV, Shure, Sennheiser dynamics
fields.notable_addition_(2023): Iconic Stax recording console acquired and installed January 5, 2023 ([samphillipsrecording.com](https://samphillipsrecording.com))
key_mics: Vintage Neumann U48; Neumann U87; 2× RCA DX77 ribbon; Altec 639B (from Les Paul estate); Altec M11 "Coke Bottle" 21D capsule tube mic; AKG 451 vintage matched stereo pair
Neumann U48, U67, 2× U47, 2× U87; 4× RCA 77DX ribbon; 3× AKG C 451E; Sennheiser 441, 4× MD421; 6× Shure SM57
Neumann U67; OG Neumann U47; 2× Royer 121; Royer SF12 stereo; AKG C4000B; Earthworks SR77 pair; Schoeps 221A; 4× Sennheiser 421; 4× Shure SM7b
key_outboard: Spectrasonics 610 Complimiter; 4× Altec 1566a tube mic pres; Altec 436C Limiter; Langevin 5116 × 2; Langevin 251A Passive EQs × 2; Vintech X73 (Neve 1073 style); UA 1176 LN; 2× UA LA3A; Tube Tech LCA2B stereo tube compressor; Gates Sta-Level tube limiter; Roland Space Echo RE-201; Audicon Plate Reverb
2× UA 1176; 2× UREI LA-4; ADL1000 tube compressor; Spectra Sonics 610; Roland Space Echo 201 (×2); Fender Reverb Tank; custom Nashville 1970s plate reverb; Fairchild Reverbatron
2× UA Classics LA2A; 2× Vintage UREI 1176 Rev H; Vintage Gates Staylevel; Drawmer stereo compressor; BAE Dual 1073; 2× Millennia HV35; Coil Audio CA-286S × 2; 2× DBX 160/160A
instruments: Hammond organ + Leslie; Fender Rhodes Suitcase 88; Wurlitzer (standard + tube model); 2× vintage Ludwig kits; Farfisa; Fender and Gibson vintage guitars; 1964 + 1973 Fender Precision Basses; Ampeg B15; 1940s Kay upright bass; Jenco vibraphone + celeste
Hammond B3; Yamaha grand piano; Wurlitzer; 2× Fender Rhodes; Fender Princeton Blackface amp; Yamaha Motiff 7; Korg Triton
Yamaha C7 Grand Piano (disklavier); '81 Gibson Les Paul Black Beauty; Fender Stratocaster; PRS SC58; multiple Fender Jazz Basses (4- and 5-string); Gibson J45
fields.key_compressors_eq: Teletronix LA-2A; UA 1176LN; 4× UREI LA-3A; Neve 2254/E; 2× DBX 160 Classic; Pultec EQP-1A; Altec 1566 tube mic pre
fields.effects: Eventide H3000; AMS RMX-16; 2× Yamaha Rev-5; Lexicon 200; Lexicon Super Prime Time
fields.key_compressors: 4× vintage Spectra Sonics 610; 2× modern V610 peak limiters; 2× POM 670 (Fairchild 670 Mk II) with vintage transformers; 4× Pulse-Tec EQs
fields.custom_unit: Green sidecar by Greg Pace/FunkWerkes: 4× Universal Audio 100D tube preamps (2 sourced from Sam Phillips Recording, 2 from FAME Recording Studios), each with vintage UA passive EQs; passive summing mixer
monitors: ProAc Studio SM100's (tri-amped with Spectra Sonics 202 system)
fields.room: 3,000 sq. ft.; real echo chamber; every room wired to patchbay; custom mid-century modern gobos/DIs/cue system

notes: Tours run daily 10am until ~7pm
Recording sessions begin at 7pm (after tours close); all recording takes place in the original room
Analog techniques + modern hybrid workflow; "blend of Sam's analog techniques and today's modern"
Full band: 5-hour minimum **$1,000** ($200 each additional hour)
Vocal recording only: 3-hour minimum **$600**
Non-refundable deposit: $200 required
Studio size: 3,000 sq ft
Ampex MM1200 2" 16-track (1970s)
Ampex MM1200 2" 24-track (1970s)
Ampex 300-3 ½" all-tube 3-track
Ampex 351 ¼" full-track mono mastering (1950s)
3× Ampex AG440 ¼" 2-track mastering (1960s)
Magnecorder Type PT6-R ¼" mono (late 1940s)

records.name: MISSISSIPPI

fields.monitoring: Soffit-mounted bi-amped Bryston-powered mains (2× TAD 15" + TAD 4001 through JBL Smith Diffraction horn, tuned with White Instruments 4400 EQs); 18" floor subs; extensive near-field options
fields.mics: Sequential serial-numbered pair Neumann U-67; valve U-47; RCA 77DX; plus Neumann, AKG, Sennheiser, A-T, Audix, Shure, EV, PML
fields.mastering_room: Fully equipped Sadie digital mastering room
fields.studio_floor: 30×35 ft + 3 isolation rooms with direct sight lines

notes: Studer 2" 16- and 24-track
Studer 1" 8-track
Studer ½" 2- and 4-track
Studer ¼" 2-track
Studer A827 1" 2-track (recommended for hot analog mix before digital)
Full kitchen; seating for 24

records.name: MUSCLE SHOALS, AL (CORRIDOR-ADJACENT)

fields.studio_x_(new): Alabama's first fully immersive mix room; developed with ADAM Audio and Glenn Rosenstein (announced 2024–2025)
fields.instruments: Yamaha C7 grand piano; Hammond A100 organ; Wurlitzer electric piano; Rhodes Mark I Stage 88

notes: Console: **Neve 8232**
Tape: MCI JH-24 2" 24-track
Outboard: 1× UREI 1176 Blackface; Sontec parametric EQ; 3× Tube-Tech compressors; **Tube EMT 140 plate reverb**
Dimensions: 15'×24' control; 24'×34' studio; up to 5 isolation booths

Console: **SSL 6000E** (reconditioned by Vince Fast and Greg Pace; Atomic Instrument power supply + THD-Labs Tangerine automation); original Studio B hand-built **Universal Audio 12-channel console** (also present)

Outboard: UTA UnFairchild 670M II; 2× Pultec EQP-1A3; Tube-Tech; Summit Audio ECS-410 Everest channel strip; 3× Empirical Labs EL8 Distressor; UREI 1176 Blackface; 2× Tree Roots/Branch preamps; Coil Audio CA-70
Available from 5pm after daytime tours (Tue–Sat); all day Sun–Mon
Recommended: start Saturday 5pm, through Monday evening
Non-refundable deposit required
Contact: recording@muscleshoalssoundstudio.org (7–14 business day response time; do NOT call the business line)
Studio musicians available for hire
Vault (original bank vault) used for guitar cabinets/amps

records.name: LITTLE ROCK / HELENA, AR

fields.delta_magic___gist_music_(helena,_ar): A 501(c)(3) nonprofit working to restore the historic Gist building in downtown Helena as a live music venue, recording studio, and speakeasy. **Not yet open as of research date** ([deltamagic.org](https://www.deltamagic.org/the-gist); [savethegist.com](https://savethegist.com/about)). Watch for future development.
fields.wolfman_studios_(little_rock,_ar): 26 Flag Road, Little Rock, AR; 501-812-8071; wolfmanrecordingstudios@gmail.com; Rates: $35/hr weekend, $25/hr weekday, $40/hr remote ([Music Connection Directory, April 2025](https://www.weekly-bulletin.com/pdf/industrycontacts/Apr25_Directory_Recording_Studios.pdf))

notes: No Helena-area recording studio taking outside clients was confirmed operational for 2024–2026.

records.name: B2. Stax Records / Stax Museum *(Memphis, TN)*
B3. American Sound Studio *(Memphis, TN — Chips Moman)*
B4. Hi Records / Royal Studios *(Memphis, TN)*
B6. Muscle Shoals Sound Studio *(3614 Jackson Hwy, Sheffield, AL)*
B10. Cosimo Matassa's J&M Studio *(New Orleans, LA)*
Studios Confirmed Active — Data Partially Missing
Corridor Geography Notes
notes: **Water Valley, MS** (Dial Back Sound) is 20 miles south of Oxford and functionally part of the Oxford/North Mississippi music community
**Como, MS** (Delta Recording Service) is approximately 50 miles north of Oxford; historically affiliated with Fat Possum / Clarksdale scene
**Florence, AL** (FAME original location) and **Sheffield, AL** (Muscle Shoals Sound) are across the Tennessee River from each other; collectively called "the Shoals"

**Baton Rouge** proper: No commercially significant studios (comparable to NOLA/Memphis) were identified. The East Baton Rouge Parish Library River Center Recording Studio is a public makerspace, not a professional commercial facility.
